Work Samples

Documentation samples across API references, conceptual guides, implementation guides, AI case studies, and UX writing.

API Documentation
API Reference

Multi-Factor Authentication API Documentation

A mock REST API reference built to demonstrate real-world API documentation skills. Covers customer self-registration, OTP verification, TOTP-based MFA enrollment, secure login flows, password recovery, and rate limiting — written for banking and finance platforms. Includes curl examples, JSON request/response schemas, caution callouts, and an interactive Swagger UI reference.

REST API OpenAPI / Swagger Authentication Flows Banking & Finance Developer Audience
View
Conceptual Guides
Conceptual Guide

Event-Driven Architecture: Conceptual Guide & Glossary

A developer-facing conceptual guide that unpacks Event-Driven Architecture from first principles — covering core terminology, Broker and Mediator topologies, event processing models, architectural patterns like CQRS and choreography, and common EDA technologies including Kafka, RabbitMQ, and Google Pub/Sub. Each concept is grounded with real-world analogies to make complex ideas accessible.

System Architecture Developer Audience Microservices Kafka / RabbitMQ Glossary
View
Implementation Guides
Implementation Guide

Application Server Deployment Guide: WildFly + Maven on Linux

A step-by-step server deployment guide written for system administrators setting up a Java application server on Red Hat / CentOS Linux. Covers JDK installation, WildFly configuration, Maven setup, Oracle OJDBC driver deployment, LibreOffice and Wkhtmltopdf integration, heap tuning, datasource configuration, and application file deployment — with exact commands for each step.

Linux / CentOS WildFly Maven Oracle DB Sysadmin Audience
View
AI & Technical Case Studies
Case Study

Jira Summarizer with Microsoft Copilot RAG: Agent Alpha

A technical case study documenting the design and build of Agent Alpha — an internal AI assistant built in Microsoft Copilot Studio using Retrieval-Augmented Generation. The agent bridges SharePoint documentation and live Jira data, allowing Product Owners, Developers, and QA teams to query product guides and get instant ticket summaries without switching tools. Covers implementation, API integration, conversation flow design, and challenges encountered.

Microsoft Copilot Studio RAG Jira API SharePoint AI Automation
View
UX Writing
UX Case Study

Designing a Session Navigator for AI Chat Interfaces

A UX writing case study proposing a collapsible session navigator for long-running AI conversations — addressing the core pain point that power users face when trying to retrieve earlier prompts in tools like ChatGPT and Claude. Covers information architecture, microcopy, wireframes, feature design, and voice and tone principles. The concept transforms AI chat from a messaging window into a structured project workspace.

UX Writing Microcopy Information Architecture AI Interfaces Wireframes

About

How I work

I am a Technical Writer who creates documentation for a wide variety of audiences — from developers and QA engineers to product owners, system administrators, end users, and customers.

What drives my work is curiosity. Before I write a single word, I dig into the system, talk to the people who build it, and understand it from the inside out. That process is what makes the difference between documentation that technically covers something and documentation that actually helps someone.

I bring strong writing, close attention to detail, and a user-centric mindset to every project. I am equally comfortable writing API references with curl examples and schema tables as I am designing UX microcopy or producing step-by-step deployment guides for sysadmins.

Audience-first approach

Every doc is shaped by who will read it and what they need to do with it.

Deep system understanding

I explore complex systems end to end before documenting them — always.

Adaptable across domains

From API docs to AI agents to UX writing — I adapt to what the work demands.

Cross-functional collaborator

I work closely with developers, PMs, designers, and QA to get documentation right.
